title: Live phone-to-laptop barcode scanning flow
description:
When scanning barcodes on the phone via QR handoff, results should appear on the laptop in real-time instead of on the phone.

Current flow: phone scans barcode → results show on phone → confirm on phone.
Target flow: phone scans barcode → ScanResult created → laptop sees it immediately → confirm on laptop.

Implementation:

1. Phone scan page behavior change: after ISBN lookup returns candidates, create a ScanResult (scan_type='isbn') and show a simple 'Sent to review queue' confirmation instead of rendering candidates on the phone. Phone stays ready for the next scan.

2. Laptop scan page live updates: add an HTMX polling region (hx-trigger='every 3s') that checks for new pending ScanResults for the current user. When a new one arrives, render candidates inline on the scan page so the user can confirm/discard without navigating to the review queue.

3. The QR token already encodes the user PK, so phone scans are attributed to the correct user via the ScanResult.scanned_by FK. No session/pairing ID needed beyond what exists.

4. After confirming a candidate on the laptop, the poll area resets to 'waiting for scans' and the phone can scan the next book.

5. Consider: should the phone auto-restart the camera after sending a scan? This would allow rapid scanning of multiple books.

Technical approach:
- New HTMX partial endpoint: GET /ingest/scan/poll/ returns pending ScanResults for current user as HTML fragment
- Laptop scan page includes a div with hx-get pointing to that endpoint, hx-trigger='every 3s'
- Phone scan page: detect if arrived via QR (e.g. session flag set by phone_scan_auth) and switch to 'scanner mode' — auto-submit to queue instead of rendering candidates
- Confirm/discard actions on the laptop post back and the poll refreshes

No new dependencies needed — HTMX polling is built in.
close_reason: done

Accessibility should be considered throughout development; this is the formal verification. Deliverable: accessibility audit report with all issues resolved, tested on desktop and mobile in both themes.
close_reason: Design polish and accessibility epic complete.

title: Add title_page_poll view and image-card partial
description:
Add the polling endpoint that desktop and phone use to discover and display pending title-page scans, plus the image-card template that renders each one.

## Changes

- `ingest/urls.py` — add `path("scan-title/poll/", views.title_page_poll, name="title_page_poll")`.
- `ingest/views.py::title_page_poll` (NEW):
  - `@login_required`.
  - Returns `ScanResult` rows for the requesting user (or all rows if staff) with `status="awaiting_ocr"` and `scan_type="ocr"`.
  - Renders `ingest/_title_page_poll.html`.
- `ingest/templates/ingest/_title_page_poll.html` (NEW):
  - Iterates over scans; renders an image card per scan via `_title_page_card.html` (or inline — small enough either way).
  - Empty state: "Waiting for photos from your phone..." text matching the barcode-page voice.
- `ingest/templates/ingest/_title_page_card.html` (NEW, or inline in poll partial):
  - Thumbnail of `scan.image` (use a max-height/width to keep cards compact).
  - Run OCR button: `hx-post="{% url run_ocr scan.id %}"`, swaps card with the OCR-results partial.
  - Discard button: `hx-post="{% url discard_title_scan scan.id %}"`, `hx-confirm="Discard this photo?"`, swaps card with empty.

## Tests

- `title_page_poll` returns only the requesting users awaiting_ocr scans.
- Staff sees all users awaiting_ocr scans (matches `review_queue` behavior).
- Empty queryset renders the empty state.
- Image src in the partial resolves to `scan.image.url`.

## Dependencies

Depends on awaiting_ocr status bead and run_ocr endpoint bead (the URL names referenced in the template).

Bead: bd-xh6 (parent epic)

title: Enrich author and subject records from authority sources
description:
Explore options for enhancing catalog metadata using external authority sources:

Authors:
- Wikidata: biographical info, dates, alternate names, links to Wikipedia articles
- LC Name Authority File (id.loc.gov): authorized forms, cross-references
- VIAF: already partially integrated for variant names; could pull more data (associated works, dates)
- NLI J9U authority records

Subjects:
- LC Subject Headings (id.loc.gov): broader/narrower terms, related subjects, scope notes
- Could enable hierarchical subject browsing (e.g., 'Jewish law' → 'Talmud' → 'Bava Metzia')

Questions to explore:
- What data is freely available via APIs?
- How to store enrichment data (additional fields on Author/Subject models, or a separate enrichment table)?
- Should enrichment happen automatically on ingest or be a manual/batch operation?
- How to display enriched data on author/subject detail pages?

close_reason: All models registered in Django admin with list displays, filters, search, inlines for identifiers, images, and series volumes.

title: Auto-extract all MARC fields on record creation from catalog lookup
description:
When a record is created via ISBN lookup or OCR cascade (selecting a candidate), the full MARC record data should be extracted and saved to all related models — not just the few fields that go through the manual entry form.

Current problem: the ISBN lookup flow passes a handful of fields as GET params to the manual entry form. This loses: additional authors, subjects, series info, all external identifiers (LCCN, OCLC, etc.), the source MARC record itself, and the publication date from the 008 field.

Fix: when a candidate is selected (either from ISBN lookup _candidates.html or OCR results), the full parsed record data should be stored (e.g. in the session) and used to populate all related models on save. Specifically:

1. Store the full parsed record dict AND the raw marcjson in the session when a candidate is selected
2. On manual_entry form save, if session has candidate data:
   - Save source_marc (marcjson) and source_catalog on the Record
   - Extract and create ALL authors (not just the first) from MARC 100 and 700 fields
   - Extract and create subjects from MARC 650 fields
   - Extract publication date from MARC 008 or 260/264
   - Extract and save external identifiers (LCCN from 010, OCLC from 035, ISBN from 020)
   - Extract series info from 490/830
3. The manual entry form still allows the user to review and override, but defaults come from the MARC data

Files to modify:
- ingest/views.py (isbn_lookup_view, manual_entry)
- ingest/templates/ingest/_candidates.html (pass full parsed data, not just a few fields)
- sources/marc.py (may need to extract 700 additional authors, 010 LCCN, 035 OCLC)
close_reason: parse_record now extracts additional authors (700), subjects (650), LCCN (010), OCLC (035). New confirm_candidate view and _attach_from_candidate function create all related models on save. Candidate data stored in session via select_candidate POST flow.

title: Add discard_title_scan endpoint with image cleanup and confirmation
description:
Add an endpoint to discard an `awaiting_ocr` scan: delete the image file from disk, set status to `discarded`, keep the row for audit. Triggered from the image card in both desktop and phone views.

## Changes

- `ingest/urls.py` — add `path("scan-title/<int:scan_id>/discard/", views.discard_title_scan, name="discard_title_scan")`.
- `ingest/views.py::discard_title_scan` (NEW):
  - `@login_required`, `@require_POST`, ownership/staff check.
  - Idempotent: if already discarded, return 200 with empty body (or the empty-state poll partial).
  - `if scan.image: scan.image.delete(save=False)` — removes the file from MEDIA_ROOT; tolerates missing file.
  - `scan.image = None`, `scan.status = "discarded"`, `scan.save()`.
  - Returns empty body (the calling card swap leaves an empty slot; the next poll cycle reconciles).
- The card template (in the poll partial bead) wires `hx-confirm="Discard this photo?"` on the Discard button.

## Why a separate view instead of overloading discard_scan

The existing `ingest.views.discard_scan` redirects to `isbn_scan` or `review_queue` — flow-coupled to barcode/ISBN. Title-page discard is HTMX-card-driven and has its own image-cleanup responsibility. Keep them separate.

## Tests

- Discard awaiting_ocr scan: file removed from MEDIA_ROOT, status=discarded, image is None on the row.
- Discard already-discarded scan: idempotent, no error.
- Auth check: non-owner non-staff → 403.
- Missing image file on disk: discard still succeeds without raising.

## Dependencies

Depends on awaiting_ocr status bead.

Bead: bd-xh6 (parent epic)

title: GitHub Actions CI pipeline
description:
Set up a GitHub Actions workflow to run on PRs and pushes to main.

Steps to run:
1. Python 3.13 setup with uv
2. Install dependencies (uv sync)
3. Lint: ruff check . && ruff format --check .
4. Unit tests: uv run pytest --ignore=tests/e2e
5. E2e tests: install Playwright browsers, uv run pytest tests/e2e/

Consider:
- SQLite is built into Python, no DB service needed
- Playwright needs browser install step (playwright install chromium --with-deps)
- Cache uv and Playwright browser downloads for speed
- No secrets needed for tests (SRU calls are mocked)
close_reason: done

title: Add GitHub link and build state indicator to site chrome
description:
Surface a link to the otzar GitHub repo (https://github.com/dchud/otzar) and a small build/state indicator in the site chrome.

## Scope

1. **GitHub link** — add to the header nav (top-right area in templates/base.html, near the dark-mode toggle and auth controls) and/or the footer. Header placement is preferred for visibility; footer is a fine fallback if the header gets crowded.
   - Use a small GitHub mark icon (inline SVG, no external icon font).
   - Open in a new tab (`target="_blank" rel="noopener"`).
   - Same focus/min-touch-target treatment as other header links.

2. **Build/state indicator in the footer** (templates/base.html) — show:
   - Short commit SHA (first 7 chars) of `HEAD` at deploy/build time.
   - Branch name if not `main`.
   - A version/tag if one is present (`git describe --tags --always`).
   - Link the SHA to the corresponding commit on GitHub (e.g. `https://github.com/dchud/otzar/commit/<sha>`).

## Implementation notes

- Resolve git info at startup, not per request. Options:
  - A management command or build step writes `otzar/_build_info.py` (or similar) with constants the template can read via a context processor.
  - Or read once in a context processor at process startup and cache in module scope.
- Local dev should still work when git info is unavailable — fall back to showing nothing or just "dev".
- For Fly.io / Docker deploys, the Dockerfile/entrypoint can capture git metadata into env vars or a generated file at build time. See `Dockerfile` and `entrypoint.sh` for the right hook point.

## Acceptance

- Header (or footer) shows a GitHub link that opens the repo in a new tab.
- Footer shows commit SHA (linked to GitHub), branch (if non-main), and tag (if present).
- Works in local dev (gracefully degrades when git metadata is missing).
- Works in deployed environment (commit SHA reflects the actually-deployed commit).
- E2e Playwright test confirms the GitHub link is present and the footer renders without error.

close_reason: MARC parser with extract_marc_records, parse_record (245, 100, 260/264, 008, 020, 880 linkage, series 490/830), has_hebrew, get_field_value, record_to_marcjson. Handles both LC 880 pattern and NLI direct Hebrew. 39 tests.

title: End-to-end integration tests with Playwright
description:
Add Playwright browser tests covering the main user workflows:

1. Public browsing: home → browse categories → author/subject detail → record detail
2. Search: search bar → results → record detail, including queries with punctuation
3. ISBN ingest flow: login → ingest → scan ISBN → see candidates → select → confirm → record exists in catalog
4. Manual entry flow: login → ingest → new → fill form → submit → record exists
5. Record management: view → edit → save → verify changes; view → delete → confirm gone
6. Auth: login required for ingest, admin link visible for staff
7. Dark mode toggle: click toggle, verify class changes

These tests would have caught the bugs found during manual testing:
- Candidate JSON not passing through the form
- NULL constraint on title_romanized
- FTS5 syntax error with punctuation
- Browse items not being clickable links
- Dark mode not toggling (Tailwind v4 config issue)
close_reason: 37 Playwright e2e tests covering: home page, browse views with clickable items, record detail, search (including punctuation), auth (login/logout), ingest landing, ISBN flow (mock), manual entry with validation, edit record, delete record, dark mode toggle with persistence. Also fixed logout to use POST form (Django 5+ requirement).

title: Add Deutsche Nationalbibliothek (DNB) as SRU source for ISBN lookup
description:
Add DNB as a third ISBN lookup source alongside NLI and LC. DNB has a fully open SRU endpoint returning MARC21, so it fits the existing parsing infrastructure with minimal changes.

Endpoint: https://services.dnb.de/sru/dnb
ISBN index: dnb.num
Record schema: MARC21-xml
SRU version: 1.1
Auth: none required

Implementation:
1. Add a dnb_client in sources/sru.py (similar to lc_client)
2. Add DNB ISBN query to isbn_lookup() in sources/cascade.py
3. Parse results with existing MARC21 parser
4. Add SRU_DNB_URL to .env.example
5. Tests for the new client and cascade integration

Context: ISBN 9781732294110 was not found in NLI or LC. DNB catalogs many international publications and would improve coverage for recent English, German, and other European-language titles.

Related: also consider BnF (UNIMARC) and Library Hub Discover (MODS) as future sources — those need different parsers.
close_reason: done

description:
Add a text field to the Record model for users to describe an item's provenance (ownership history, inscriptions, bookplates, etc.).

Requirements:
1. New TextField on Record, nullable/blank
2. Searchable via the existing search infrastructure
3. Displayed on the record detail page below primary metadata elements
4. Editable in the record form and admin

title: Explore sources for book cover images
description:
Investigate free/open sources for book cover images to display in the catalog.

Potential sources:
- Open Library Covers API (covers.openlibrary.org): free, no auth needed, look up by ISBN, OCLC, or LCCN. Returns JPEG in S/M/L sizes. Probably the best option for broad coverage.
- Google Books API: thumbnail images available via ISBN lookup, no auth for basic use, but terms of service may restrict caching/storage.
- Internet Archive: covers available for books in their collection, accessible via Open Library IDs.
- HathiTrust: page images for digitized volumes, but access restrictions vary.
- HebrewBooks.org: title page images for Hebrew texts in their collection (we already have experience scraping these from experiments).
- Publisher/distributor feeds: unlikely to be useful at this scale.

Questions to explore:
- Which sources have the best coverage for Hebrew religious texts specifically?
- Should we fetch and store cover images locally, or hotlink to external URLs?
- If storing locally, how does this interact with our image storage scheme (media/title-pages/)?
- Should covers be a separate model/field from title page scans?
- Display: where to show covers (record detail, browse lists, search results)?
- Fallback: what to show when no cover is available (the title page scan if we have one, or a generic placeholder)?
close_reason: done

title: Review gate: catalog views
description: Verify: all browse views render with real data, record detail shows all fields/related items/image/MARC, browse counts and pagination correct, series shows held/gaps, search returns results in Hebrew/English/romanized, RTL rendering correct, typography readable, all view tests pass.
close_reason: All browse views, record detail, search, home page complete with tests. RTL/bidi rendering and typography in place. 211 tests passing, lint/format clean.

title: Add pytest-socket to block live HTTP in tests
description:
The test suite mocks all external HTTP calls (SRU, VIAF, Open Library) at the function level via @patch, but there is no global safety net. If a new test forgets to mock, it will hit a real server.

Add pytest-socket as a dev dependency to block all outgoing network by default during test runs. Tests that need real network (none currently) would use @pytest.mark.enable_socket.

Steps:
1. uv add --dev pytest-socket
2. Verify all existing tests still pass (they should — none make real connections)
3. If any test fails, it means we found an unmocked live call — fix it

This is a one-line dependency add with zero code changes expected.
close_reason: done

title: Richer test dataset with diverse records
description:
Create a more comprehensive test/demo dataset with ~25 records covering a variety of:

- Languages: English, Hebrew, Aramaic, Yiddish, German, French
- Eras: ancient (pre-1500), early modern (1500-1800), modern (1800-1950), contemporary (1950+)
- Genres: Talmud, Halacha, philosophy, history, literature, reference
- Series: at least one multi-volume set with gaps
- Authors: single author, multiple authors, editors, translators
- Sources: some with MARC data (NLI, LC), some manually entered

Could pre-download MARC records from NLI/LC for well-known texts (Talmud, Mishneh Torah, Shulchan Aruch, Guide for the Perplexed, etc.) and store them as test fixtures, avoiding live API calls during testing.

This would replace/extend the current load_test_data management command.
close_reason: done

comments.author: dchud
comments.text: Let's focus on modern and contemporary eras - this isn't a rare materials collection.

title: Defer OCR in title_page_upload and add run_ocr endpoint
description:
Decouple title-page image upload from OCR execution. The upload step saves the image and creates a `ScanResult` in `awaiting_ocr`; a new `run_ocr` endpoint runs Claude Vision on demand and returns the existing edit-metadata partial.

## Changes

- `ingest/views.py::title_page_upload` (modify):
  - Save uploaded image bytes to `ScanResult.image` (Django ImageField; existing `staging/%Y/%m/%d/` upload_to).
  - Status `awaiting_ocr`, `ocr_output=None`, `candidate_records=[]`.
  - Do NOT call `extract_metadata_from_image`.
  - Stop writing to `tmp/title_pages/<uuid>.jpg` (redundant once `ScanResult.image` is the source of truth).
  - Phone-mode response (`request.session.get("phone_scan_target") == "title"`): render a small "sent" partial that shows the uploaded image plus Run OCR / Retake controls.
  - Desktop-mode response: render the new `_title_page_poll.html` card partial (the desktop pane will swap it in).
- `ingest/urls.py` — add `path("scan-title/<int:scan_id>/ocr/", views.run_ocr, name="run_ocr")`.
- `ingest/views.py::run_ocr` (NEW):
  - `@login_required`, `@require_POST`, ownership/staff check.
  - 409 if `scan.status != "awaiting_ocr"`.
  - Read image bytes via `scan.image.read()`; call `extract_metadata_from_image`.
  - On success: set `scan.status = "pending"`, `scan.ocr_output = metadata`, save. Render existing `_ocr_results.html` (which already supports the metadata edit + cascade-search step).
  - On `extract_metadata_from_image` returning None: leave `awaiting_ocr`; return error partial inline (with retry-and-discard affordances).

## Tests

- Upload phase: creates `ScanResult` with image saved, status `awaiting_ocr`, no OCR called (mock at the import boundary, since `pytest-socket` blocks live HTTP).
- `run_ocr` happy path: state transitions correctly, ocr_output populated, partial rendered.
- `run_ocr` 409 on already-processed.
- `run_ocr` OCR-None: state unchanged, error partial rendered.
- Auth check on both endpoints.

## Dependencies

Depends on the `awaiting_ocr` status bead (so the status value exists).

Bead: bd-xh6 (parent epic)

title: Review gate: catalog models
description: Verify: all migrations apply cleanly on fresh database, all model tests pass (creation, validation, relationships, ID generation), FTS5 search returns expected results for multilingual test records, admin is usable for all models, record IDs are unique and correctly prefixed.
close_reason: All migrations apply cleanly, 32 model/search tests pass, FTS5 works for multilingual records, admin usable for all models, record IDs unique and correctly prefixed. Lint and format clean.

title: RTL and bidi rendering
description: Cross-cutting across all views: dir=auto on mixed-content elements, dir=rtl on known Hebrew/Aramaic blocks. Tailwind RTL utilities (rtl: variant). Test with real Hebrew content — verify alignment, punctuation placement, mixed-script lines.
close_reason: Bidi template tags (bidi_text, bidi_auto) with Hebrew detection. CSS rules for RTL text-align and unicode-bidi. Skip-to-content link and aria-label on nav. 11 tests.

title: Update scan-title template: desktop QR sidebar + poll pane and phone streamlined view
description:
Restructure `title_page_scan.html` so it has two render modes (desktop with QR + poll, phone streamlined), mirroring the existing `isbn_scan.html` structure. ## Changes - `ingest/views.py::title_page_scan` (modify): - Pass `phone_scan_target` (and/or `phone_scanner`) into the template context. - `ingest/templates/ingest/title_page_scan.html` (restructure): - Top-level `{% if phone_scan_target == "title" %}` branches to phone view; else desktop view. - **Desktop branch**: - Existing direct upload form (kept verbatim). - Right sidebar with QR image: ``. Hidden on small screens (matches barcode page). - "Incoming photos" pane: `
`. - **Phone branch**: - Streamlined "Take title page photo" capture using `` (existing markup). - After upload, the poll partial is swapped in showing the just-uploaded card; user picks Run OCR or Retake. - Lightweight poll on the same endpoint to detect desktop action. ## Tests - E2e (in the e2e bead): desktop variant renders QR sidebar; phone variant renders capture-only UI; switching `phone_scan_target` toggles the variant. - Unit: GET `/ingest/scan-title/` with no session flag → desktop variant context. With `phone_scan_target=title` → phone variant context. ## Dependencies Depends on: - QR/auth flow bead (for `?target=title` support and `phone_scan_target` session key). - title_page_poll bead (for the `title_page_poll` URL and partial). - defer-OCR bead (so upload returns the new card partial). Bead: bd-xh6 (parent epic)

close_reason: Subject model with heading, romanized heading, source, M2M to Record.

Only SECRET_KEY and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY required to start locally.

title: Home page
description: Main entry point for community members. Prominent search bar. Quick links to browse views. Summary stats: total records, authors, recent additions. Keep it simple.
close_reason: Home page with search bar, browse category links, summary stats, recent additions. Uses home view with dynamic data.

title: Ingest workflows
description: Data input for catalogers: manual entry form, ISBN/barcode scanning, title page OCR via Claude Vision, authority matching on ingest, series workflow, review queue, cross-device QR handoff, ingest session state. All usable from phone browsers. Deliverable: cataloger can add records via any method with series, authority matching, review queue, and cross-device handoff working.
close_reason: All ingest tasks and review gate complete.

title: Management command to find and remove orphaned entities
description:
Create a management command (e.g. cleanup_orphans) that finds Authors, Publishers, Subjects, Locations, and Series with no linked records and optionally deletes them.

Usage: uv run python manage.py cleanup_orphans [--dry-run] [--delete]

Default (no flags): list orphans with counts.
--dry-run: same as default, just list.
--delete: actually delete orphaned entities.

This is useful after bulk record deletions or data cleanup.
close_reason: Added to Django admin: record count column, orphan filter, and delete-orphans action for Author, Subject, Publisher, and Location admins.

title: JavaScript barcode scanning from camera
description:
Add real-time barcode scanning via the phone/laptop camera to the ISBN scan page (ingest/templates/ingest/isbn_scan.html).

Current state: The page has a manual ISBN text input that POSTs to the isbn_lookup HTMX endpoint. The camera viewfinder area shows a placeholder message.

Implementation:

1. Add a JavaScript barcode library. Two options:
   - Barcode Detection API (navigator.ml.barcodeDetector): native in Chrome and Safari on mobile, zero dependencies, but not available in Firefox. Check for support and fall back gracefully.
   - QuaggaJS (or its maintained fork @nicolo-ribaudo/quagga2): works everywhere, heavier (~100KB). Use as fallback when Barcode Detection API is unavailable.

   Recommendation: try Barcode Detection API first, fall back to QuaggaJS.

2. Camera access: use navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({ video: { facingMode: 'environment' } }) to get the rear camera on phones. Display the live feed in a <video> element in the viewfinder area.

3. Scanning loop: continuously capture frames from the video feed and pass them to the barcode detector. On successful detection of an EAN-13 or ISBN barcode, extract the ISBN string.

4. On detection: auto-fill the ISBN input field and trigger the HTMX POST to isbn_lookup (same as manual entry). Show a brief visual confirmation (green flash or checkmark) so the user knows the scan worked.

5. Permissions UX: if camera permission is denied, show a clear message and keep the manual input available as fallback. The manual ISBN input should always remain visible below the camera viewfinder.

6. Mobile considerations: the camera viewfinder should be large enough to frame a barcode easily (~300x200px minimum). Add a torch/flashlight toggle button if the browser supports it (via MediaStreamTrack.applyConstraints with torch: true).

7. The JS can be inline in the template or in a separate static file (static/js/barcode-scan.js). Keep it simple — no build step.

Files to modify:
- ingest/templates/ingest/isbn_scan.html (add video element, JS scanning logic)
- Possibly static/js/barcode-scan.js (if extracted to separate file)
- No Python changes needed — the backend ISBN lookup already works.
close_reason: done

title: Place of publication browse view
description:
Add a browse view for place of publication at /browse/places/. Group records by their place_of_publication field, show record counts. Links go to a filtered list of records from that place.

Note: this is different from the Location browse, which shows physical shelf locations. Place of publication comes from MARC 260$a/264$a and is stored as a text field on Record, not a separate model. The browse view will need to aggregate by distinct values of record.place_of_publication.

May want to normalize place names (e.g. 'Boston :' vs 'Boston') since MARC data often includes trailing punctuation.
close_reason: done

title: Safe update procedure
description: Deploy process: pre-deploy backup (automatic), pull code, install/update deps, run migrations, collect static, restart server. Non-destructive migrations only — review each migration for data safety. Media files never touched by deploys. Documented rollback path via pre-deploy backup.
close_reason: Safe updates via GitHub Actions CD: push to main → test → deploy → entrypoint migrate → gunicorn. Volume persists across deploys. Rollback via fly deploy --image. Non-destructive migration policy documented in implementation plan.

title: Review queue

Queue view with expandable candidate details and source MARC. Confirm creates Record from selected candidate. Discard marks for cleanup. Staging cleanup management command with configurable retention (e.g. 30 days).
close_reason: Review queue with pending/confirmed/discarded status, expandable candidate details, confirm creates Record, discard marks for cleanup. Staff sees all scans. Cleanup management command with 30-day retention. 19 tests.

Create base.html template with: HTML lang attribute, dir handling for bidi, Tailwind/HTMX/Alpine script tags, navigation skeleton (home, browse, search, ingest), dark mode (prefers-color-scheme default, Tailwind dark: utilities, user toggle), responsive viewport meta.

title: E2e Playwright test for phone-to-desktop title-page handoff
description:
End-to-end coverage for the full handoff workflow, per CLAUDE.md (user-visible workflow changes require Playwright tests in `tests/e2e/`).

## Test plan

Two browser contexts in one test, simulating desktop + phone. The phone context navigates a generated `phone-auth/<token>/` URL directly (bypasses real QR scanning).

### Test 1 — happy path

1. Desktop context: log in, GET `/ingest/scan-title/`, assert QR sidebar + empty poll pane present.
2. Generate a phone-auth token (sign on the test side using the same `TimestampSigner` and `target=title`).
3. Phone context: navigate `/ingest/phone-auth/<token>/`, asserted to land on the streamlined capture UI.
4. Phone context: upload a fixture title-page JPG via the file input.
5. Desktop context: poll picks up the new card (assert thumbnail and Run OCR / Discard buttons appear within ~5s).
6. Desktop context: click Run OCR. Patch `extract_metadata_from_image` (and the cascade search) at the boundary — `pytest-socket` blocks live HTTP, so all external calls must be stubbed. Assert metadata edit UI renders.
7. Continue through cascade search and record creation; assert `Record` row exists.

### Test 2 — discard flow

1. Repeat steps 1–4 from Test 1.
2. Desktop context: click Discard, confirm the prompt.
3. Assert: image file no longer present in MEDIA_ROOT, scan row has status=discarded.
4. Phone context: poll detects scan no longer awaiting → reverts to capture UI.

## Fixtures

- Small JPG under `tests/fixtures/title_pages/` (a few KB, no real bibliographic content).

## Dependencies

Depends on all other child beads in this epic.

Bead: bd-xh6 (parent epic)

title: Dark mode verification
description: All pages in both light and dark modes. Contrast ratios pass in both. No hard-coded colors that break in dark mode. Title page images: subtle border/background against dark backgrounds.
close_reason: Dark mode: all templates verified for dark: variant coverage on borders, backgrounds, text. Focus ring offsets for dark mode.

title: VIAF client
description: Port from experimental experiment_viaf.py. Search VIAF by author name with cascade: exact Hebrew → romanized → broad keyword → multi-word AND. Parse cluster responses: extract VIAF ID, J9U ID, LCCN, authorized headings, variant name forms. Cluster matching logic: normalize and compare headings/variants. Return structured enrichment data. Test with known authors.
close_reason: VIAF client with search cascade, cluster parsing, cluster matching with normalization, viaf_enrich for best-match authority lookup.

title: Project scaffolding
description: Set up the Django project with all infrastructure: project config, dependencies, .env template, frontend tooling (Tailwind/HTMX/Alpine), auth and admin, testing infrastructure. Deliverable: styled placeholder page with navigation, auth, dark mode, and passing tests.
close_reason: All scaffolding tasks and review gate complete.

title: Consistent optional record metadata: provenance, bookplates, stamps, dedications
description:
Epic covering optional metadata elements users can attach to catalog records: provenance notes, bookplate text/images, stamp text/images, and dedication text/images.

Goals:
1. Consistent data model for optional text+image metadata on records
2. Unified input UX — easy to add, consistent across all element types
3. Unified storage and search — all text fields indexed and searchable
4. Consistent rendering on item pages — text shown below primary metadata, images shown below cover images, elements hidden when no values present
5. Phone camera capture for image uploads across all image-capable elements

Subtasks: bd-epd (provenance), bd-efh (bookplates/dedications), bd-e02 (stamps)

title: WCAG contrast audit for dark mode
description:
Review all templates for contrast ratio issues in dark mode specifically. The initial WCAG audit may not have caught all issues since dark mode class-based toggling wasn't working at the time.

Check:
- Text on dark backgrounds meets WCAG AA minimum (4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text)
- Gray-on-gray combinations (e.g. text-gray-400 on bg-gray-900) may be too low contrast
- Link colors in dark mode (blue-400 on gray-900)
- Form input borders and placeholder text visibility
- Status badges (NLI/LC source labels, held/gap indicators)
- Error/warning message contrast in dark mode
- The site-wide password page (inline styles, not Tailwind)

Test with browser dev tools contrast checker or axe-core in both modes.
close_reason: done

title: Catalog data models
description: Core schema: Record, Author, Subject, Publisher, Series/SeriesVolume, Location, ExternalIdentifier, TitlePageImage models. Record ID generation (base62 with configurable prefix). Admin registration. FTS5 full-text search setup. Deliverable: migrations run, models visible in admin, sample records can be created and searched.
close_reason: All catalog models, admin, FTS5, and review gate complete.

title: Ingest session state
description: Preserve work-in-progress across the three input methods. If barcode fails and cataloger switches to OCR, data carries over. Options: Django session storage or HTMX client-side state accumulation. Decide during implementation.
close_reason: Session state for ingest is handled through Django sessions (source_marc, source_catalog) and GET parameter pre-fill. Data carries across method switches via the existing ISBN→manual and OCR→manual flows.

title: Series and SeriesVolume models
description: Series: title, title_romanized, total_volumes (nullable), publisher (FK, nullable). SeriesVolume (through model): series FK, record FK, volume_number (string for non-numeric like '3a', 'supplement'), held (boolean, default True for gap representation).

description: Before saving a new Record, check if author name matches or nearly matches existing Author using VIAF cluster data (variant names). Prompt cataloger: 'This looks like the same author as N other items. Confirm?' Option to link to existing Author or create new. Same pattern for Publisher (exact match).
close_reason: Authority matching with exact, romanized, and variant name matching. Normalize for comparison. HTMX partial for author match prompts. 26 tests.

title: Testing infrastructure

close_reason: pytest configured with pytest-django, conftest.py, smoke tests for all three apps passing.

title: Record identifier generation
description: Implement base62-encoded ID with configurable prefix. Site settings model (singleton) or Django settings for prefix string (default 'otzar-'). Utility function that generates the next ID from a sequence.
close_reason: Base62 encoding and configurable prefix record ID generation implemented.

title: In-browser rotation and cropping for title-page images before OCR
description:
Add a small editor on the image-card UI: rotate 90°/180°/270° and crop. Applied client-side before "Run OCR" submits, or server-side if simpler.

Why: improves OCR accuracy for off-axis or padded captures without requiring a retake.

Bumped from epic bd-xh6 follow-ups.

title: Visual coherence review
description: Consistent spacing, alignment, typographic hierarchy across all pages. Dense pages (record detail, browse lists) reviewed for scannability. Mixed-script pages reviewed for visual balance.
close_reason: Visual coherence: consistent spacing, typography, border colors across all templates. Browse cards and record detail reviewed for scannability.

title: Title page OCR via Claude Vision
description: Frontend: camera capture input, client-side resize to ~1500px via JS canvas, HTMX upload with progress. Backend: store in staging, send to Claude Vision API with OCR prompt (structured JSON output, no nikkud, ALA-LC romanization, gematria date conversion). Display extracted metadata for review/correction. On confirm run search cascade, show candidates, select or proceed with OCR data alone. Attach title page image to Record.
close_reason: Claude Vision OCR with structured JSON extraction, gematria date handling, ALA-LC romanization prompt. Title page scan/upload views with HTMX, client-side resize. Search cascade integration. 16 tests.

title: Response caching for external queries
description: Port caching strategy from experiments. Cache key: hash of base URL + query params. Store in Django-managed location (DB table or file cache). Cache lookup before network requests. Time-based expiry (configurable, e.g. 30 days) or manual invalidation.
close_reason: Response cache using Django file-based cache with MD5 key generation, TTL support, invalidation.

title: Phone landing screen with both barcode and title-page capture options
description:
Currently the phone-auth target is fixed by the QR (`target=isbn` or `target=title`). Add a phone landing page that lets the user pick either workflow once authenticated, so a single QR works for whichever scan they want next.

Considerations:
- Could replace target-specific redirect with a generic phone landing page.
- Or add a "switch mode" link from each phone-mode page.
- Affects qr_code_view, phone_scan_auth, and both phone scan templates.

Bumped from epic bd-xh6 follow-ups.

title: Record detail page
description: Display all Record fields and related models. Title page image. Related items: same author, same series (with gap indicators), same location. Commentary chain links where subject data supports it. Expandable source MARC in tagged display format. External identifier links (LCCN, NLI, VIAF). Bidi rendering with dir=rtl/auto. URL: /catalog/{record_id}/{slug}/.
close_reason: Record detail page with bidi rendering, authors/subjects/publishers/locations, series volumes with gap indicators, expandable MARC display, external identifier links, related works. 6 tests.

title: Search cascade engine
description: General cascade mechanism: takes catalog identifier and metadata dict, runs progressively broader queries (defined per catalog), stops at first query returning results, returns parsed MARC records. NLI and LC cascade definitions are configuration (data structures), not code. NLI: 7-query cascade. LC: 4-query cascade.
close_reason: Search cascade engine with NLI (7-step) and LC (4-step) cascades as data structures. Skips steps with missing fields, stops at first hit. 20 tests.

title: Clean trailing MARC punctuation on ingest
description:
MARC data often includes trailing punctuation that's part of the cataloging format but looks wrong in display: 'Boston :' (trailing colon+space from 260$a), 'Harvard Business School Press,' (trailing comma from 260$b), 'Brown, John Seely.' (trailing period from 100$a), 'Duguid, Paul,' (trailing comma from 700$a).

Add a cleanup step during ingest that strips common trailing MARC punctuation from field values before storing them. Affects: title, author names, publisher names, place of publication, subjects.

Common patterns to strip:
- Trailing ' :', ' ;', ' /', ','
- Trailing '.' except when it's part of an abbreviation or initial (e.g. keep 'Jr.' but strip 'Duguid, Paul.')
- Leading/trailing whitespace

Apply during _attach_from_candidate and in the confirm_candidate view when creating the Record. Consider also applying retroactively to existing records via a management command.
close_reason: done

title: Record model
description: Central catalog model. Fields: record_id (base62 with configurable prefix, CharField), slug (Unicode, allow_unicode=True for Hebrew/Aramaic), title, title_romanized, subtitle, date_of_publication (integer year, nullable), date_of_publication_display (freeform string for approximate dates), place_of_publication, language, source_marc (JSON/marcjson, nullable), source_catalog (NLI/LC/null), notes, created_by (FK User), created_at, updated_at.
close_reason: Record model with all fields, auto ID generation, Unicode slug, date display fallback.

title: Health check and monitoring
description: /health/ endpoint returning 200 if app and DB accessible. Log access and rotation. Optional uptime monitoring via external ping service.
close_reason: Health check at /health/ already implemented. fly.toml configures Fly.io health checks at 30s intervals. fly logs for log access.

title: Catalog search
description: Search bar in site navigation (every page). FTS5 full-text search across all indexed fields. Results page with key fields highlighted. Faceted filtering by author, subject, date range, location, language (progressive enhancement). Cross-language: Hebrew and romanized queries find the same record.
close_reason: FTS5 search view with pagination, result highlighting, cross-language search. 7 tests.

title: NLI ingest not storing source_marc or external identifiers
description:
Records ingested via NLI ISBN lookup have no source_marc data and no ExternalIdentifier records (ISBN, LCCN, OCLC, etc.). Discovered when fetch_covers skipped a Heschel record (otzar-b) ingested from NLI -- it had no identifiers to look up covers with.

The Social Life of Information (otzar-4, ingested earlier) does have identifiers stored, so the issue may be specific to a code path or a regression.

To investigate:
1. Check confirm_candidate and _attach_from_candidate -- are source_marc and identifiers being set correctly?
2. Check what the NLI SRU response actually returns for ISBN 9780374513313
3. Compare with the working record (otzar-4) to see what differs
4. Check if the candidate session data includes the MARC fields that _attach_from_candidate expects

This blocks cover image lookups and any future feature that relies on stored identifiers or MARC data.
close_reason: done

title: Developer documentation
description: Audience: contributors. Getting started (clone, deps, .env, migrate, superuser, dev server). Project structure (what each app does). Architecture overview (sources → ingest → catalog data flow). Data model reference. SRU/VIAF integration (cascade, caching, rate limiting). OCR integration (Claude Vision, prompt, caching). Testing (run tests, fixtures, live integration tests). Coding conventions.
close_reason: Developer docs: getting started, project structure, architecture, data model, external sources, OCR, search, testing, linting, Justfile commands. 303 lines.

title: Extend QR/auth flow to support per-target scan landing
description:
Extend the existing QR + phone-auth handoff so the QR code can direct the phone to either the ISBN scanner or the title-page scanner.

## Changes

- `ingest/views.py::qr_code_view` — accept `?target=` query param (allowed values: `isbn`, `title`; default `isbn`). Sign token as `f"{user_pk}:{target}"`.
- `ingest/views.py::phone_scan_auth` — parse the new token format. Set `request.session["phone_scan_target"]` to the parsed target. Redirect to `isbn_scan` for `isbn`, `title_page_scan` for `title`. Backwards compatible: tokens with only `<user_pk>` (no colon) default to `isbn` so any QR a user already loaded keeps working.
- Keep the existing `phone_scanner` session flag for now (phone-mode template branch); `phone_scan_target` carries the more specific intent.

## Validation

- Unknown target → HTTP 400.
- Expired / malformed token unchanged behavior (existing 400/403).

## Tests (`ingest/tests.py`)

- Token with `isbn` target → redirects to isbn_scan, sets session flag.
- Token with `title` target → redirects to title_page_scan, sets session flag.
- Token with no target (legacy format) → defaults to isbn.
- Token with unknown target → 400.
- Expired token → 403 (existing behavior preserved).

## Out of scope

UI for selecting target on the desktop side (`?target=title` is wired by the title-page scan template in a sibling bead).

Bead: bd-xh6 (parent epic)
close_reason: Token now signs user_pk:target; phone_scan_target session key set; legacy tokens default to isbn; tests cover all parsing branches

title: Phone-to-desktop handoff for title page scanning
description:
Add a QR-driven phone-to-desktop handoff for the title-page scan ingest path, mirroring the existing barcode/ISBN scan flow. The user opens /ingest/scan-title/ on their laptop, scans a QR code with their phone to authenticate and land on a streamlined capture UI, takes a title-page photo, and reviews the image — from either device — before OCR runs. Confirmation kicks off OCR and proceeds into the existing edit-metadata + cascade-search workflow.

## Goals

- Smooth desktop → phone → camera → desktop loop with one tap each side.
- Image is reviewed (and can be discarded/retaken) before OCR tokens are spent.
- Either device can confirm or discard; the other catches up via polling.
- The existing direct-upload path on desktop stays as an alternative.

## High-level flow

```
Desktop /ingest/scan-title/  ──QR──►  Phone /phone-auth/<token>/
                                           │ sets phone_scan_target=title
                                           ▼
                                      Phone scan-title (streamlined)
                                           │ take photo, upload
                                           ▼
                                  ScanResult.image saved, status=awaiting_ocr
                                           │
                  ┌────────────────────────┴────────────────────────┐
                  ▼                                                 ▼
         Desktop poll: image card                       Phone shows image card
         [Run OCR] [Discard]                            [Run OCR] [Retake]
                  │
                  ▼
        Either side: Run OCR ──► OCR runs, status=pending,
                                 ocr_output set, edit-metadata UI shown
                                 (existing cascade search continues)

        Either side: Discard ──► image file deleted, status=discarded
                                 (confirmation prompt before delete)
```

## Data model

Extend `ScanResult.STATUS_CHOICES` with a new value `awaiting_ocr` (OCR-only):

| Status         | image | ocr_output | candidate_records | Meaning                                     |
|----------------|-------|------------|-------------------|---------------------------------------------|
| awaiting_ocr   | set   | NULL       | []                | (NEW) Image uploaded, OCR not yet run       |
| pending        | set   | set        | populated         | OCR done, awaiting record selection         |
| confirmed      | —     | —          | —                 | Record created                              |
| discarded      | NULL  | —          | —                 | Rejected; image file deleted, row kept      |

Image storage moves from `tmp/title_pages/<uuid>.jpg` to `ScanResult.image` via the existing `ImageField` (already configured for `staging/%Y/%m/%d/`). DB migration required.

## URLs / views

| URL                                      | View                       | Notes |
|------------------------------------------|----------------------------|-------|
| `GET /ingest/qr/?target=title`           | `qr_code_view` (extended)  | Token signs `<user_pk>:<target>`; target ∈ {isbn, title}. |
| `GET /ingest/phone-auth/<token>/`        | `phone_scan_auth` (ext.)   | Sets `phone_scan_target` session key; redirects accordingly. |
| `GET /ingest/scan-title/`                | `title_page_scan` (ext.)   | Renders desktop (QR sidebar + poll pane) or phone (streamlined) variant. |
| `POST /ingest/upload-title/`             | `title_page_upload` (chg.) | Defers OCR. Saves image to `ScanResult.image`, status=awaiting_ocr. Returns phone confirmation in phone mode, image-card partial otherwise. |
| `GET /ingest/scan-title/poll/`           | `title_page_poll` (NEW)    | Returns image cards for the current users awaiting_ocr scans. Polled by desktop and phone. |
| `POST /ingest/scan-title/<id>/ocr/`      | `run_ocr` (NEW)            | Runs OCR on saved image, transitions to pending, returns existing `_ocr_results.html` partial. 409 if already processed. |
| `POST /ingest/scan-title/<id>/discard/`  | `discard_title_scan` (NEW) | Deletes image file (`scan.image.delete(save=False)`), status=discarded. Idempotent. `hx-confirm` on the trigger. |

All write endpoints use `@login_required` and check `scan.scanned_by == request.user or request.user.is_staff`.

## QR token format

Currently signs `str(user_pk)`. Extend to `f"{user_pk}:{target}"`. Backwards compatible: tokens missing the target default to `isbn` so any in-flight QR keeps working.

## UI

**Desktop `/ingest/scan-title/`** (when not phone_scanner):
- Existing direct upload form retained.
- New right sidebar with QR code (mirrors barcode page; `target=title`).
- New "Incoming photos" pane polling `title_page_poll` every 3s.
- Each pending scan: thumbnail + Run OCR / Discard buttons. Discard uses `hx-confirm="Discard this photo?"`.
- Run OCR swaps the card with the existing edit-metadata partial; the cascade-search flow takes over from there unchanged.

**Phone `/ingest/scan-title/`** (when `phone_scan_target=title`):
- Streamlined capture using the existing `<input capture="environment">` UI.
- After upload, replaces form with image card + Run OCR / Retake. Retake calls discard then re-renders capture.
- Lightweight poll on the same endpoint to detect desktop action and avoid stale UI.

## Reuse vs new

- `_scan_poll.html` polling pattern is reused with a new template `_title_page_poll.html` for image cards.
- `_ocr_results.html` partial unchanged — `run_ocr` returns it directly so the cascade-search step is identical to today.
- New `discard_title_scan` rather than overloading existing `discard_scan` (which has ISBN-flow-coupled redirects).

## Error handling & edge cases

- **Upload failure**: phone shows inline error, capture stays available, no partial `ScanResult` created.
- **OCR failure** (`extract_metadata_from_image` returns None): scan stays in `awaiting_ocr`; card shows error with Retry / Discard.
- **Race (both devices act)**: last write wins. `run_ocr` returns 409 if no longer in `awaiting_ocr`. `discard` is idempotent. Both polls reconcile.
- **Token expiry / wrong target**: `TimestampSigner` already validates max_age=3600. Missing target → isbn (back-compat). Unknown target → 400.
- **Image cleanup**: `scan.image.delete(save=False)` on disk; `image=None`, `status=discarded` in DB; row kept for audit.
- **Stale awaiting_ocr rows**: out of scope for v1; consider a janitor command later.
- **Phone session conflict**: `phone_scan_target` overwritten on each `phone-auth` hit.

## Testing

Per CLAUDE.md, user-visible workflow changes require Playwright e2e coverage.

**Unit tests** (`ingest/tests.py`):
- QR token round-trip (valid, expired, malformed, missing target → isbn default).
- Deferred-upload: status=awaiting_ocr, image saved, OCR not invoked.
- `run_ocr`: happy path, 409 on already-processed, OCR-None error, auth check.
- `discard_title_scan`: file deleted, status set, idempotent, auth check.
- `title_page_poll`: returns only requesting users awaiting_ocr scans.

**E2e tests** (`tests/e2e/`):
- Two browser contexts (desktop + phone); phone navigates a generated `phone-auth` URL.
- Phone uploads fixture title-page image → desktop poll → desktop Run OCR (Claude Vision patched at boundary, since `pytest-socket` blocks live HTTP) → metadata edit → cascade search (also patched) → record created.
- Phone uploads → desktop discards (with confirm) → image gone, status=discarded, phone poll reverts to capture.

Fixture: small JPG under `tests/fixtures/title_pages/`, no real bibliographic content.

## Out of scope (potential follow-ups)

- Multi-page (title + verso) capture and merged OCR.
- Janitor command to prune stale awaiting_ocr rows.
- In-browser rotation / cropping before OCR.
- Phone landing screen offering both barcode and title-page capture from one QR.

## Child beads

This epic is implemented through these child beads (created with this epic):
1. Extend QR/auth flow to support per-target scan landing.
2. Add awaiting_ocr status + use ScanResult.image for title-page uploads.
3. Defer OCR in title_page_upload; add run_ocr endpoint.
4. Add title_page_poll view and image-card partial.
5. Update scan-title template: desktop QR sidebar + poll pane; phone streamlined view.
6. Add discard_title_scan endpoint with image cleanup + confirmation.
7. E2e Playwright test for full handoff flow.

title: Mobile optimization
description: Test all flows on phone-sized screens (especially ingest). Touch targets min 44x44px. Camera interactions tested on iOS Safari and Android Chrome. Image upload resize verified on mobile browsers.

title: Add awaiting_ocr status and use ScanResult.image for title-page uploads
description:
Add a new explicit status `awaiting_ocr` to `ScanResult` and start populating the existing `image` ImageField for title-page uploads. Sets the foundation for deferred OCR.

## Changes

- `ingest/models.py::ScanResult.STATUS_CHOICES` — add `("awaiting_ocr", "Awaiting OCR")`.
- Generate Django migration.
- Document the state machine in a docstring on `ScanResult` (per CLAUDE.md doc-style: factual and concise):
  - `awaiting_ocr` — image saved, OCR not yet run (OCR scans only).
  - `pending` — candidates ready, awaiting record selection.
  - `confirmed` — record created from a selected candidate.
  - `discarded` — rejected by user; image file deleted on transition.

## Tests

- Migration applies cleanly.
- Existing fixtures/queries that filter `status="pending"` still behave correctly (no rows incorrectly matched).

## Out of scope

The actual write to `ScanResult.image` happens in the deferred-upload bead; this bead only adds the status value, the migration, and docs so the next bead has the column available.

Bead: bd-xh6 (parent epic)
close_reason: Added awaiting_ocr status, max_length=20, migration 0002, model docstring, and 3 unit tests

Built with Material for MkDocs.

title: Verify pagination is visible on all browse and search views
